Khoros is a global leader in digital-first customer engagement software. We build enterprise software for digital customer service, online brand communities, and social media management — differentiated by award-winning services with 20+ years of experience. Over 2,000 brands, including 52 of the Interbrand 100 companies, use Khoros to create customers for life. Khoros has over 10 industry awards from TrustRadius, Stevie Awards, G2, and more. Khoros, built from Spredfast and Lithium, is part of Vista Equity Partners with nine offices globally.

# Khoros
Unclaimed
Digital customer engagement platform
Social Media ManagementMarketing AutomationCommunity Platforms
Visit Website 
4.0(252 across the web)
PaidVisit Website 
## TL;DR - Khoros
* Enterprise platform combining online communities, social media management, and digital customer service
* AI-powered omnichannel support with intelligent routing across social, chat, SMS, email, and phone
* Used by Microsoft, Samsung, Cisco, and 300+ large brands for customer engagement at scale
**Pricing:** Paid only
**Best for:** Enterprises & pros
**4.0/5** across review platforms
## Pros & Cons
Pros
* Unified platform for community, social, and customer service
* Proven at enterprise scale with 300+ major brand deployments
* AI-powered routing and automation reduce agent workload significantly
* Community self-service deflects support tickets at scale
* Comprehensive omnichannel coverage from one interface
* Strong governance and compliance features for regulated industries
Cons
* No public pricing — enterprise sales process required
* Steep learning curve due to platform breadth
* Primarily designed for large enterprises, not SMBs
* Implementation can take months for full deployment
* Interface can feel dated compared to newer competitors
## Ratings Across the Web
4(252 reviews)

G2252 reviews
4/5

Ratings aggregated from independent review platforms. Learn more
## Preview
Khoros preview
## Key Features
Branded online community with forums, Q&A, and knowledge baseAI-powered customer service with intelligent routing and prioritizationOmnichannel support across social, chat, SMS, email, and phoneSocial media content publishing and schedulingGovernance and compliance controls for social mediaCommunity gamification and reputation systemsAdvanced analytics across all engagement channelsAutomated chatbots and self-service deflectionAgent efficiency tools with AI-assisted responsesEnterprise-grade security and single sign-on
## Pricing
Paid
Khoros offers paid plans. Visit their website for current pricing details.
View pricing 
## About Khoros
By Toolradar Team·Updated Mar 19, 2026
Khoros is an enterprise customer engagement platform that combines online community management, social media management, and digital customer service into a unified solution. The Communities product lets brands build self-service support forums, discussion spaces, and knowledge bases that reduce support ticket volume — Zoom's community alone drives 8.6M page views. Khoros Service provides AI-powered agent tools with intelligent routing, automated prioritization, and omnichannel

results.title: From Tactics to Strategy: The evolution of community use cases
results.text:
From Tactics to Strategy: The evolution of community use cases

EXPERT INSIGHTS

May-09-2025

# From tactics to strategy: The evolution of community use cases

Todd Nilson

Todd Nilson has nearly 25 years of experience as a community strategist, helping organizations like SAP and Greenspace build stronger, more authentic connections within their communities. He is the founder of Clocktower Advisors, co-founder of TalentLed, and president of the Community Consultants Collective.

## From tactics to strategy: The evolution of community use cases

I’ve noticed a common pattern as a consulting strategist helping enterprise communities. Many start with a singular focus—typically customer support—but never grow beyond it. While this approach delivers value, it leaves so much potential untapped.

These communities need a broader vision. Online communities are capable of far more than answering support questions. They are ever-changing ecosystems that can fuel growth, build loyalty, spark innovation, and boost retention–when leaders decide to make the right investments.

Think of community use cases like a series of branching paths on a map. Each path you open, whether it’s adding onboarding resources, creating spaces to suggest product ideas, or launching advocacy programs, unlocks new value and opens the door to even more possibilities. What begins as a simple way to save money on customer support calls can transform into a powerful engine for product feedback, customer-led marketing, or even faster sales cycles.

Let’s make these potential transformations easier to understand. Here are four main groupings of value-driving use cases:

Support & Operational Efficiency: This is where most companies begin their community journey, seeing quick wins through fewer support calls and smoother customer onboarding.

Marketing & Growth: These communities boost your brand visibility, provide social proof when customers share success stories, and turn happy users into advocates who bring others on board.

Innovation & Product Development: Communities in this category help you hear customer needs more clearly, build better products, and respond to market changes faster.

Revenue & Customer Retention: Here, communities drive loyalty, help customers get more value from your products, and speed up sales—all contributing directly to your bottom line.

I’ll provide real-world examples of each use case category in the following sections. You'll see how communities can grow from serving a single purpose to becoming strategic assets that deliver value across your organization.

## Support and operational efficiency: The most common starting point

For many enterprise organizations, community strategy begins with a practical goal: reduce support costs. And it works! I’ve seen firsthand how peer-to-peer support forums, searchable knowledge bases, and staff-moderated Q&A threads consistently deflect tickets and free up internal teams.

Consider the findings from Aberdeen

results.title: Khoros Communities solution overview
results.text:
Khoros Communities solution overview

Khoros is now part of the IgniteTech family. Learn More.

#### SOLUTION ASSET

# Khoros Communities solution overview

Khoros empowers businesses to reduce service costs, boost customer lifetime value, and drive revenue growth with its enterprise-class online community platform. Discover why Khoros Communities boasts more Fortune 100 success stories than any other provider and how you can scale engagement with fewer resources.

- Download overview here

### Ready to build a customer community that lasts?

In this solution overview, learn how Khoros next-gen community management software enables companies to build and scale communities faster and with far fewer resources by providing:

Expert design made simple: Pre-built templates and drag-and-drop design make building an enterprise-class community and scale engagement quick and easy.

Best-in-class management tools: With AI-enhanced moderation and workflow automation, you can spend more time on strategy and less on routine tasks.

Leading customer engagement features: Expertly designed interaction styles, leading gamification features, and impactful analytics fuel continuous interaction and prove ROI.

results.title: Khoros: Complete Review
results.text:
Khoros: Complete Review

Last updated: 5 months ago

3 min read

59 sources

Affiliate Disclosure

Khoros positions itself as an enterprise-focused AI social media management platform that unifies community, care, and marketing functions into a single operational framework. Recent acquisition by IgniteTech in May 2025 introduces new ownership dynamics, though it remains too early to assess long-term impact on product development or customer support [[42]] [[47]].

The platform targets Fortune 1000 organizations requiring sophisticated governance capabilities and cross-channel integration, evidenced by documented enterprise client implementations including General Motors and Midco [[52]] [[53]]. Khoros differentiates through supervised AI learning that requires administrative approval for AI-generated content, positioning human oversight as a core architectural principle [[45]].

Key capabilities center on multilingual brand monitoring, unified community-service integration, and enterprise-scale governance features. Customer evidence validates operational outcomes in specific implementations, though broader performance claims require verification due to citation accessibility challenges affecting multiple case studies.

Target audience fit appears strongest for large organizations with dedicated IT resources capable of managing complex integrations and 8-12 week implementation timelines [[53]] [[56]]. Budget requirements reportedly start at $150,000 annually, positioning Khoros outside typical SMB consideration [[54]].

Bottom-line assessment reveals a platform with documented enterprise success in specific implementations, balanced against implementation complexity, significant investment requirements, and mixed customer feedback regarding interface usability. Organizations evaluating Khoros should prioritize proof-of-concept testing given the documented gap between vendor capabilities and operational reality affecting 43% of AI marketing tool implementations [[48]].

## Khoros AI Capabilities & Performance Evidence

Core AI functionality encompasses supervised learning systems that require administrative approval for AI-generated responses, differentiating from pure automation approaches [[45]]. The platform reportedly offers AI Discovery Defender for maintaining community content visibility in AI search results and AI Brand Defender for correcting misinformation in AI-generated answers [[42]]. Multilingual capabilities reportedly span extensive language coverage, though specific metrics require verification due to inaccessible supporting documentation [[40]] [[45]].

Performance validation shows measurable outcomes in documented customer implementations. Midco achieved 460% increase in CSAT survey completion rates and 73% call deflection through Apple Messages for Business integration [[53]]. The same customer
